channel.title: Craig's Daily Newsfeed
channel.description: A fresh list of notable items every day, with links and one-paragraph summaries.

item.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
item.description: The US-Iran war sharply escalated after an Iranian missile-and-drone attack on a base in Jordan killed two US soldiers (with one missing) — the conflict's first US combat deaths on the ground. US forces struck Iran's Revolutionary Guard for an eighth straight night to 'punish' the attack, while Iran fired drones at US forces in Kuwait; officials say 16 US service members have now died in the war. ⚠ Active war escalating toward wider conflict; casualty figures still emerging

item.title: Russia launches largest ballistic missile attack on Kyiv since start of war
item.description: Russia hit Kyiv with what Ukraine called its largest ballistic missile attack since the war began, killing at least one person and wounding more than a dozen. Ukraine is especially exposed to ballistic strikes amid a shortage of Patriot interceptor munitions. ⚠ Casualty counts still being confirmed

item.title: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
item.description: 'Manosphere' influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan were arrested in Miami by US Marshals after UK prosecutors filed additional charges; the Crown Prosecution Service has requested their extradition. Their lawyer maintains they are innocent. The case has drawn heavy international attention to how the US handles the extradition request. ⚠ Charges are allegations; defendants deny wrongdoing

item.title: Jammu Kashmir में भारी बारिश से बाढ़ जैसे हालात; श्री अमरनाथ यात्रा स्थायी रूप से रोकी गई
item.description: The state broadcaster reports flood-like conditions in Jammu & Kashmir after heavy rain, and that the Amarnath Yatra pilgrimage has been permanently halted for the season — with the government-response framing typical of DD News. ⚠ State-media source

item.title: 'हमें नई ज़िंदगी दीजिए या इच्छामृत्यु': राजस्थान में मां बनने के बाद डायलिसिस पर पांच महिलाएं
item.description: Five women in Rajasthan who gave birth at government hospitals have written to President Droupadi Murmu demanding either 'a new life or euthanasia' after their health collapsed post-delivery, leaving them on dialysis. The independent outlet spotlights a wrenching maternal-health failure and demand for accountability. ⚠ Sensitive health story; alleges systemic hospital failures

item.title: 比利时拟禁止进口以色列非法定居点产品
item.description: Chinese state media reports that Belgium's government announced it will draft a trade ban prohibiting imports of products from illegal Israeli settlements — coverage that reflects Beijing's readiness to amplify European moves critical of Israel. ⚠ State media — selective amplification of anti-Israel measures

item.title: Погрузку нефти на терминале КТК в Черном море остановили после атаки БПЛА
item.description: The state agency reports that oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium (CPC) terminal in the Black Sea was halted after a drone attack — and, in a companion item, stresses that no oil spill occurred, a framing that downplays the strike's impact. ⚠ Russian state-media framing minimises the damage

item.title: Украинские беспилотники атаковали два танкера в морском терминале КТК в Новороссийске
item.description: Meduza reports Ukrainian drones struck two tankers during oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium's marine terminal in Novorossiysk — the same strike RIA covers, but framing it as a direct hit on tankers rather than a contained incident.

item.title: スタンフォード大 佐々木麟太郎 大リーグ・マーリンズ入り表明
item.description: Stanford University's Rintaro Sasaki — drafted by both Japanese pro baseball and MLB, making his choice closely watched — announced at a press conference in Hanamaki, Iwate, that he will sign with the MLB Miami Marlins.

item.title: 殺人事件の現場を地域つなぐ存在に 特殊清掃業者が開いた子ども食堂
item.description: A human-interest piece from the independent daily on a specialist crime-and-death-scene cleanup company that opened a children's cafeteria (kodomo shokudo), turning a site associated with a murder into a place that helps knit the local community together.

item.title: Asbestos complicates Royalton mill demolition
item.description: The demolition of a mill in Royalton has been delayed by asbestos, dismaying residents who'd been told the structure would be gone by spring. Last fall the Royalton Selectboard gave property owner Eric Jacobs 120 days to remove it.

item.title: The boricuas keep reigning at Centrobasket! Puerto Rico wins its fifth straight title
item.description: Puerto Rico's women's basketball team beat Mexico in the final to claim its fifth consecutive Centrobasket regional title — extending a run of dominance for the boricua squad.

item.title: The report oil companies are worried about: climate attribution science
item.description: A new National Academies report finds that the science of attributing specific weather damages to climate change is maturing (though still with limits) — a development the fossil-fuel industry is watching warily, since firmer attribution could strengthen climate-liability lawsuits.

item.title: Mathematicians still don't know the fastest way to multiply numbers
item.description: Despite decades of progress and clever algorithms, mathematicians still haven't pinned down the theoretically fastest way to multiply large numbers — a surprisingly deep open problem at the heart of computation.

item.title: Speech recognition and text-to-speech in less than 500kb
item.description: Moonshine's 'micro' project packs both speech recognition and text-to-speech into under 500kb — a striking demonstration of how compact on-device voice models can now be, opening the door to voice interfaces on tiny, offline hardware.

item.title: Crunchyroll reveals English dub casts for new seasons of Mushoku Tensei, 100 Girlfriends, You and I Are Polar Opposites
item.description: Crunchyroll unveiled English dub casts for new seasons of Mushoku Tensei, The 100 Girlfriends Who Really, Really, Really, Really, Really Love You, and You and I Are Polar Opposites — with all three dubs debuting Sunday.

item.title: Tune in to the Midnight Heart anime's 2nd season reveals more cast, 2027 debut
item.description: The second season of 'Tune in to the Midnight Heart' revealed additional cast — Ami Koshimizu and Kaede Hondo join as Arisu's sisters — and set a 2027 debut.

item.title: US strikes hit Iran for seventh consecutive night
item.description: US forces struck Iran for a seventh straight night as the conflict ground on, with Iran retaliating against US allies in the region — Kuwait reported a power-and-water plant was hit, and a seventh night of strikes was said to have cut water to villages in Iran's south. The tit-for-tat is exposing the water and energy vulnerability of the dry Gulf. ⚠ Active war; both sides' strike claims are unverified

item.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
item.description: The EU proposed relaxing its emissions-trading system to give companies more time to cut their carbon output — a notable softening of the bloc's flagship climate policy amid competitiveness and cost pressures. Environmental groups warn it risks blunting Europe's decarbonization push. ⚠ Contested climate-policy rollback

item.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
item.description: Trump threatened new tariffs on Canada, blaming it for wildfire smoke sending 'filthy' air into US cities and claiming poor forest management costs the US billions. Canadian PM Mark Carney countered that both countries share responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say is worsening the wildfire conditions behind the smoke. ⚠ Frames a climate-driven event as a trade grievance

item.title: Vikram-1 | Skyroot | Mission Aagaman — India's First Private Orbital Rocket
item.description: The state broadcaster celebrates Skyroot Aerospace's launch of Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket — a 40-tonne, 20-metre vehicle able to carry satellites to orbit — framed as a landmark for India's private space sector in DD News's achievement-focused register. ⚠ State-media source — celebratory framing of a national milestone

item.title: सोनम वांगचुक को पुलिस ने जंतर मंतर से हटाया, पत्नी ने कहा- मैं करूंगी मार्च का नेतृत्व
item.description: Delhi police cleared activist Sonam Wangchuk — on hunger strike some 20 days — from Jantar Mantar, citing High Court directions, as doctors reported his potassium levels had dropped; his wife vowed to lead the march in his place. The independent outlet centers the civil-liberties stakes of the forced removal, a story the government has sought to downplay. ⚠ Civil-liberties dispute; framing differs sharply from state coverage

item.title: 印尼国家经济委员会主席：中印尼投资合作前景广阔
item.description: Chinese state media highlights the chair of Indonesia's national economic council saying China's support is indispensable and that China-Indonesia investment cooperation has broad prospects — a characteristic showcase of Beijing's economic-diplomacy narrative in Southeast Asia. ⚠ State media — promotional economic-diplomacy framing

item.title: Губернатор Тамбовской области посетил пострадавших от удара ВСУ по складу
item.description: The state agency reports the Tambov region governor visiting those injured in a Ukrainian drone strike on a warehouse — coverage that foregrounds Russian civilian victims and official response to the attack. ⚠ Russian state-media framing of the same strike

item.title: Украинские дроны атаковали склады Wildberries в двух регионах России; в Тамбовской области погибли семь сотрудников
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck Wildberries logistics warehouses in Russia's Tambov and Moscow regions overnight on July 18, killing seven company employees in Kotovsk (Tambov) and wounding more than 20 in the Moscow region. The independent outlet reports the same strike RIA covers, detailing the toll.

item.title: 京アニ放火殺人事件から7年 スタジオ跡地で追悼式 京都
item.description: Seven years on from the Kyoto Animation arson that killed 36 employees, bereaved families and colleagues gathered at the former studio site in Kyoto for a memorial service, offering prayers for the victims.

item.title: 皇室典範の「男系男子」に息苦しさ 「生むも継ぐも、本来は自分で」
item.description: The independent daily runs a reflective piece on the suffocating weight of the Imperial House Law's 'male-line male' succession rule, voicing the view that childbearing and succession should ultimately be one's own choice — a critical, personal counterpoint to the recently passed reform. ⚠ Independent outlet critical of the succession framework

item.title: FBI Most Wanted Suspect Captured in Alburgh
item.description: Nitish Kaushal, an FBI Most Wanted suspect, was captured in the small northern-Vermont town of Alburgh. He could face a life sentence over alleged involvement in extortion, kidnappings, murders, and international drug and weapons trafficking.

item.title: Milestone in animal health: Puerto Rico's first teaching veterinary hospital ready to open
item.description: Puerto Rico is set to open its first teaching veterinary hospital, at the Ana G. Méndez University School of Veterinary Medicine in Gurabo. The 31,000-square-foot facility required a multimillion-dollar investment and marks a milestone for animal health and veterinary training on the island.

item.title: First atmosphere found on an Earth-like planet in a distant star's habitable zone
item.description: Astronomers report detecting an atmosphere around an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone of a distant star — a significant step in the search for potentially life-supporting worlds, since a stable atmosphere is considered a key prerequisite for habitability.

item.title: TP-Link Kasa cameras leaked home GPS via unauthenticated UDP for 6 years
item.description: Researchers found that TP-Link Kasa cameras exposed users' home GPS coordinates over an unauthenticated UDP service for about six years — a serious privacy flaw letting anyone on the network (or reachable over the internet) locate a camera owner's home. ⚠ Long-standing IoT privacy vulnerability

item.title: San Francisco orders Apple, Google to remove 'nudify' apps from app stores
item.description: San Francisco's city attorney ordered Apple and Google to pull AI 'nudify' apps from their stores, arguing the companies likely made millions in fees from apps used to generate non-consensual explicit imagery. It's a notable escalation in pressure on platform gatekeepers over AI-enabled abuse. ⚠ Concerns non-consensual explicit-image generation

item.title: An older Eri thanks her heroes in My Hero Academia: 'I Am a Hero Too' anime short
item.description: A new My Hero Academia anime short, 'I Am a Hero Too,' shows a now-grown-up Eri — years after the Season 4 school-festival concert arc — thanking the heroes who saved her.

item.title: Fate/kaleid liner Prisma Illya sequel film 'Licht: Nameless Girl' to reveal new details July 20
item.description: The Fate/kaleid liner Prisma Illya franchise's official account says the sequel film 'Licht: Nameless Girl' will reveal new information on July 20.

item.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
item.description: The US pressed on with a sixth straight night of strikes on Iran, with Tehran saying bridges and civilian infrastructure were hit and casualties reported. US forces also boarded a ship in the Strait of Hormuz as the campaign widened, deepening fears of a broader escalation across the Gulf. ⚠ Active war; casualty and target claims from both sides unverified

item.title: China's economy grows at 4.3%, one of its lowest rates on record
item.description: China's economy expanded just 4.3% in the three months to June — below the government's 4.5–5% target and among the weakest quarterly readings since official GDP reporting began in the early 1990s. The worse-than-expected figures intensify concerns about a lopsided, slowing Chinese economy with global ripple effects.

item.title: Trump alleges 'shocking vulnerabilities' in US election security ahead of midterms
item.description: In a primetime address, Trump alleged 'shocking vulnerabilities' in US election security and accused China of meddling in the 2020 election — claims that directly contradict past US intelligence findings and for which he offered no evidence. The speech, days ahead of the midterms, drew scrutiny over reviving baseless voter-fraud narratives. ⚠ No evidence provided; contradicts US intelligence findings

item.title: PM Modi ने Jind से ₹14,700 करोड़ की विकास परियोजनाओं का किया शुभारंभ | H2 Train
item.description: The state broadcaster covers PM Modi launching ₹14,700 crore of development projects from Jind, Haryana — headlined by India's first hydrogen train — with the celebratory, achievement-focused framing typical of DD News. ⚠ State-media source — celebratory framing of a government event

item.title: इसरो से इतनी बड़ी संख्या में वैज्ञानिक इस्तीफ़ा क्यों दे रहे हैं?
item.description: ISRO has changed its rules for accepting resignations after an estimated 100–120 scientists left the space agency over the past year, many joining private space startups. The piece probes the brain-drain behind the move — a scrutiny of institutional strain that contrasts with state media's triumphant space coverage. ⚠ Independent framing of a problem state media downplays

item.title: 中国驻比利时使馆和驻欧盟使团联合举办建军节招待会
item.description: China's embassy in Belgium and its mission to the EU jointly hosted a reception in Brussels marking the 99th anniversary of the People's Liberation Army. The item is a characteristic piece of Chinese state diplomacy coverage — showcasing military-anniversary outreach on European soil. ⚠ State media — promotional coverage of official diplomacy

item.title: Совфед одобрил закон о разработке, внедрении и регулировании ИИ в России
item.description: Russia's Federation Council approved a law on the development, deployment, and regulation of artificial intelligence in the country. The state agency frames it as a step toward domestic AI governance amid Moscow's broader tech-sovereignty push.

item.title: Российские войска нанесли удар по Одессе. Повреждены 11 жилых домов, погибли два человека
item.description: A Russian missile strike on Odesa overnight on July 17 damaged 11 residential buildings and killed two people, with six wounded including three children, per the city's military administration. The independent outlet foregrounds the civilian toll of the attack.

item.title: 皇族数確保に向けた改正皇室典範 参議院本会議で可決・成立
item.description: The upper house passed and enacted a revised Imperial House Law to shore up the shrinking imperial family, allowing female royals to remain in the family after marriage and permitting male-line descendants of former imperial branches to be adopted (though adoptees themselves won't hold succession rights, their sons would). It's the first substantive revision of the law since 1949, passed over opposition from several parties; the ban on female emperors remains.

item.title: 急浮上した養子案、皇室の方々に戸惑い 男系男子の継承と向き合い
item.description: The independent daily reports that the suddenly-advanced adoption plan — bringing male-line descendants into the imperial family — has bewildered members of the imperial household as they grapple with male-line succession. It offers a more skeptical, human angle on the same reform NHK reports as passed. ⚠ Independent outlet surfaces unease around the reform

item.title: He's the high bailiff of Orange County, Vermont. Now he's in Iran — and saluting its 'holy war' with the US
item.description: Christopher Helali, the elected (largely ceremonial) high bailiff of Orange County, Vermont, invoked his office in social-media posts from Iran during the state funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei — including a selfie in front of a large red banner reading 'Kill Trump' and messaging endorsing Iran's 'holy war' with the US. ⚠ Involves a sitting local official endorsing violent rhetoric

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers closer to intervening in LUMA Energy vegetation-clearing projects
item.description: The US Army Corps of Engineers is moving closer to taking over vegetation-clearing work along LUMA Energy's power lines in Puerto Rico, though none of the entities involved has specified which sectors would be assigned. It's the latest turn in the island's long-running struggle to stabilize its fragile electrical grid.

item.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
item.description: The EU will require Google to share search data with rivals and open up AI features on Android, a significant antitrust-driven intervention. Google warns the changes could endanger user privacy and security; critics see it as a major crack in the company's search-and-mobile dominance.

item.title: Kimi K3: Open Frontier Intelligence
item.description: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, billed as an open, frontier-class model — the latest in a wave of powerful open-weights releases from Chinese labs that keep narrowing the gap with closed frontier systems.

item.title: Trump teleprompter aide made $100,000 betting on what Trump would say, reports say
item.description: A Trump teleprompter operator reportedly made about $100,000 on prediction markets by betting on what Trump would say in speeches, with Kalshi saying it flagged the apparent insider trading. The episode raises fresh questions about information asymmetry and integrity on political prediction markets. ⚠ Alleged insider trading, per reports

item.title: I'm Dating a Dark Summoner Anime's Teaser Video Reveals More Staff
item.description: A teaser video for the 'I'm Dating a Dark Summoner' anime revealed additional staff; the series premieres in October with HIDIVE streaming.

item.title: Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves game trailer reveals season 3's new characters, July 23 launch
item.description: A new trailer for Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves revealed its third season of characters and a July 23 launch, with Rick Strowd arriving in July, Duck King in August, Kim Kaphwan in September, and Laocorn in November.

item.title: Iran targets military bases as US launches wave of strikes
item.description: The US-Iran war escalated overnight, with explosions heard across Iran shortly after Gulf states began reporting attacks. The US launched a fresh wave of strikes while Iran targeted military bases in the region, deepening a conflict that has already disrupted global oil shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. ⚠ Active war; casualty and damage claims from both sides are unverified

item.title: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
item.description: France's National Assembly approved a landmark assisted-dying bill after years of contentious debate. The law would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict eligibility criteria, marking one of the most significant social-policy shifts in France in years. ⚠ Ethically divisive; safeguards and scope contested

item.title: China and Xi favored over U.S. and Trump in many nations: Survey
item.description: A new Pew Research survey finds that across many countries, more people now view China and Xi Jinping favorably than the United States and Trump — a shift driven both by improved perceptions of China and sharply declining views of the US. It's a striking marker of how America's global standing is being reassessed abroad.

item.title: India की पहली Hydrogen Train तैयार, जींद-सोनीपत रूट पर दौड़ेगी
item.description: The state broadcaster showcases India's first hydrogen-powered train, reported ready to run on the Jind-Sonipat route — framed as a milestone for Indian Railways and the government's clean-energy push, in the promotional register typical of DD News. ⚠ State-media source — celebratory framing of a government initiative

item.title: पटना हाई कोर्ट- सलवार उतारने का प्रयास रेप की कोशिश नहीं, टिप्पणी पर सुप्रीम कोर्ट हुआ नाराज़
item.description: The Patna High Court ruled that grabbing a woman's chest and attempting to remove her salwar do not amount to attempted rape — a finding that drew sharp displeasure from the Supreme Court. The story spotlights a contentious judicial reasoning and the higher court's rebuke, the kind of scrutiny of institutions independent outlets foreground. ⚠ Sensitive subject; judicial reasoning widely criticised

item.title: 报告：所谓“南海仲裁案”裁决是一面之词，一股逆流，一张废纸
item.description: Chinese state media promotes a newly released Beijing report arguing that the 2016 South China Sea arbitration ruling is 'one-sided, a countercurrent, a scrap of waste paper' and does not represent international law. The framing reflects China's continued rejection of the tribunal's decision on its maritime claims. ⚠ State media advancing Beijing's official legal position

item.title: Британия разработает новую баллистическую ракету для Украины, пишут СМИ
item.description: The state agency reports, citing media, that Britain will develop a new ballistic missile for Ukraine — coverage that emphasises Western escalation and foreign arming of Kyiv, a recurring frame in Russian state reporting on the war. ⚠ Russian state-media framing of Western involvement

item.title: Россия нанесла ракетный удар по Киеву. Два человека погибли
item.description: Russian forces struck Kyiv with missiles overnight on July 16, killing two people and wounding six, including a 16-year-old, according to the city's military administration and mayor Vitali Klitschko. The independent outlet centers the civilian toll of Russia's attack — a counterpoint to state coverage's focus on Ukrainian strikes.

item.title: 国産AI開発会社 エヌビディアと連携 次世代システム導入へ
item.description: Noetra, a new company pursuing home-grown Japanese AI, will adopt Nvidia's latest next-generation chips and systems to accelerate development of 'physical AI' — the foundation technology for autonomously operating robots and machines. NHK frames it as a step toward Japanese technological self-reliance.

item.title: 戦後初の再審制度見直し、改正刑訴法が成立へ 施行後5年ごと再検討
item.description: Japan is set to enact a revised Code of Criminal Procedure overhauling its retrial system — the first such reform since World War II — with the law to be re-examined every five years after taking effect. The independent daily treats it as a landmark change to how wrongful convictions can be reopened.

item.title: Ben & Jerry's Foundation says it will shut down amid legal dispute with parent company
item.description: The Ben & Jerry's Foundation says it will shut down amid an escalating legal fight with parent company Unilever over the ice cream maker's social mission. The closure puts roughly $600,000 in annual philanthropic support to Vermont organizations at risk.

item.title: 'Public administration doesn't work, politics ate it': experts analyze the DDEC scandal
item.description: El Nuevo Día convened legal and governance experts to dissect the controversy engulfing the DDEC (economic development department), centered on officials Sebastián Negrón and Francisco Domenech. The specialists argue Puerto Rico's public administration has been hollowed out by politics, turning its back on residents' real problems.

item.title: Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
item.description: A new Windows zero-day dubbed 'HiveLegacy' surfaced on the same day Microsoft shipped a record-size Patch Tuesday. Researchers describe it as a 'powerful primitive' likely capable of a range of malicious actions, raising the urgency of the month's already unusually large patch load. ⚠ Active zero-day disclosed

item.title: xAI sues a man for using Grok to generate CSAM 'deepfakes'
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is suing a South Carolina man, Terry Wayne Harwood, who it alleges knowingly circumvented Grok's safeguards to alter non-consensual images and generate and distribute child sexual abuse material. The suit is a notable move by an AI company to pursue a user for abusing its model. ⚠ Subject matter: alleged CSAM generation

item.title: Sheetz is quitting VMware, migrating 11,000 virtual machines
item.description: Convenience-store chain Sheetz is moving 838 stores and about 11,000 virtual machines off VMware to StorMagic, citing too much uncertainty since Broadcom's acquisition. It's another prominent enterprise defection illustrating the post-Broadcom VMware exodus.

item.title: Hiroyasu Kobayashi, SAFEHOUSE, AREA35 Unveil Tiny Metal: Zero Line Anime
item.description: A new anime, Tiny Metal: Zero Line — from Hiroyasu Kobayashi with studios SAFEHOUSE and AREA35 — was unveiled, with the pilot episode getting a world premiere at Japan Expo in France on July 9.

item.title: Tuesdays with Stories #633 — Puerto Rican Fattlesnake
item.description: A Skankfest-themed episode: Mark and Joe recap the festival's ups and downs, Joe steps out on Schtick or Treat, and Mark recounts doing a set in a sauna. (From the show's back catalog surfacing in the feed.)

item.title: Very Bad Wizards #305 — Emile Is the Name of the Goat (with Paul Bloom)
item.description: Paul Bloom joins David and Tamler to break down the Severance season finale and season 2 — which they agree is a return to form — then turn to an evolutionary account of the 'ick.' (A catalog episode surfacing as the feed backfills.)

item.title: Making Sense #453 — AI and the New Face of Antisemitism
item.description: Sam Harris speaks with computer scientist Judea Pearl about causality, artificial intelligence, and a resurgent antisemitism. (A catalog episode surfacing as the feed backfills.)

item.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
item.description: One day after vowing to charge cargo ships a 20% toll for using the Strait of Hormuz, Trump abandoned the plan as US Central Command reimposed a naval blockade on ships entering and leaving Iranian ports. The reversal, amid continued tit-for-tat strikes, is being read as a sign Washington is struggling to force an end to its war with Iran and to break Tehran's grip on the waterway. ⚠ Active US-Iran war; both sides' claims are contested

item.title: 'God is punishing the politicians': anger at earthquake response grows in Venezuela
item.description: Public fury is mounting over the Venezuelan government's handling of twin earthquakes that killed nearly 4,500 people, raising the threat of social unrest on top of fallout from a US military intervention. Nicolás Maduro's politician son was heckled while touring a half-destroyed social-housing project, with one grieving mother berating him on camera over the lack of disaster aid. ⚠ Death toll and aid figures are still emerging

item.title: In the aftermath of deadly shootings, ICE pauses most traffic stops
item.description: US Immigration and Customs Enforcement will suspend most non-urgent vehicle stops after two deadly shootings in under a week, according to Maine Sen. Angus King's office. The pause follows fatal incidents in Maine and Florida in which officers shot drivers of moving vehicles, drawing sharp criticism and feeding an intensifying national debate over immigration enforcement tactics.

item.title: Today's Top Headlines | Morning News | India-UK CETA | Iran-US War | 15/7/2026
item.description: The state broadcaster's morning bulletin leads on the India-UK CETA trade agreement and the ongoing Iran-US war, alongside domestic headlines. As with most DD News output, the framing emphasises government initiatives and official positions. ⚠ State-media source — framing favours the government line

item.title: 'ये लड़ाई चुनी हुई सरकार की जवाबदेही के लिए है': सोनम वांगचुक के समर्थन में कौन-कौन सेलिब्रिटी आया
item.description: Environmental activist Sonam Wangchuk is on hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of minister Dharmendra Pradhan, and several film-industry figures have come out in support. Wangchuk frames the protest as a fight for the accountability of the elected government — a story of civic dissent that state outlets largely ignore. ⚠ Independent framing of a protest state media downplays

item.title: 伊朗：只要美国袭击继续，该地区就不会出口“一滴石油”和天然气
item.description: China's state news agency amplifies a declaration from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps that, as long as US attacks continue, the region will not export 'a single drop' of oil or gas. The prominent, uncritical relay of Tehran's threat reflects Beijing's tilt toward Iran's narrative in the conflict. ⚠ State media amplifying Iran's official messaging

item.title: Бойцы "Севера" за ночь уничтожили четыре огневые группы ВСУ
item.description: The state agency reports that Russia's 'North' grouping destroyed four Ukrainian fire teams overnight — a routine battlefield-success dispatch sourced to the Russian military. Such claims are issued daily and presented without independent confirmation. ⚠ Russian state-media battlefield claim, unverified

item.title: «У Китая хорошие шансы превратить Россию в гигантский Пакистан»
item.description: Meduza retells a Wall Street Journal analysis arguing that four-plus years of war and isolation have tilted the Russia-China relationship decisively toward Beijing, turning Putin from a figure Xi once admired into a junior partner and supplicant. Both sides project a durable equal alliance, but the piece stresses the partnership's weak foundations and the many latent grounds for friction.

item.title: 皇族数の確保に向けた皇室典範改正案 参議院で審議入り
item.description: A bill to shore up the shrinking number of Imperial Family members entered deliberation in the upper house. Chief Cabinet Secretary Kihara explained a provision allowing male-line descendants of former imperial branches to be adopted into the family, stressing it would not bring in people unrelated by blood and would rest on the free will of both adoptee and adoptive parents.

item.title: 皇位の男系継承は「2600年以上の伝統」か 識者が与党幹部に異論
item.description: As the ruling party advances its imperial-succession bill, experts push back on the leadership's claim that unbroken male-line succession is a tradition '2,600 years and more' old, disputing the historical basis for that framing. The piece offers an independent counterweight to the official narrative around the same reform NHK covers. ⚠ Independent outlet challenges the 'tradition' framing

item.title: Young People Want to Leave Vermont Over Affordability Concerns, Poll Finds
item.description: A new poll finds many young Vermonters want to leave the state over cost-of-living concerns, capturing the scale of Vermont's affordability crisis and the challenge it poses to an aging state trying to attract and keep younger residents.

item.title: Contralora señala que el Prits opera sin plan estratégico y con fallas administrativas
item.description: Puerto Rico's comptroller reports that PRITS — the island's information technology and innovation service — has been operating without a strategic plan and with administrative failures, and faults it for weak oversight of agencies' cybersecurity plans. The audit adds to accountability concerns over how the government manages its digital infrastructure.

item.title: Microsoft's Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
item.description: Researchers found that old, forgotten 'shims' Microsoft never revoked make Secure Boot bypasses trivial — meaning a foundational PC boot-security feature has effectively been defeatable for most of its existence. The disclosure raises the prospect of stealthy bootkit-style attacks until the stale certificates are cleaned up. ⚠ Security disclosure — bypass reported as simple

item.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
item.description: A group of 26 former Meta employees is suing, alleging the company used a 'constellation' of internal AI tools rather than human judgment to decide who to cut — disproportionately targeting workers on medical or disability leave. Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems. ⚠ Allegation in a lawsuit; Meta denies it

item.title: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
item.description: New York became the first US state to impose a one-year moratorium on new data-center construction, unsettling an AI industry racing to expand compute capacity. Backers frame it as a blueprint for a broader anti-data-center movement over energy, water, and community-impact concerns.

item.title: Crunchyroll Store to Become Exclusive to Mega/Ultimate Fan Tiers in August
item.description: Starting in August, the Crunchyroll Store will be limited to Mega and Ultimate Fan subscription tiers, though existing open orders — including items released after the transition — will still be processed. The change effectively puts merch access behind the pricier membership levels.

item.title: The Angel Next Door Spoils Me Rotten Anime Gets English Dub
item.description: All 12 episodes of the popular rom-com 'The Angel Next Door Spoils Me Rotten' are getting an English dub, launching Wednesday, as Crunchyroll also adds more series to its manga catalog.

item.title: Tuesdays with Stories #664 — Hoofenmouth
item.description: New episode: Joe flubs a framing mission, and Mark races to make Ari Shaffir's going-away party before Shaffir moves to the UK. The usual loose, riff-heavy Tuesday.

item.title: US wants Iran to pledge to stop shooting at ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Washington is pressing Tehran to publicly commit to keeping the Strait of Hormuz open and to stop firing on passing vessels, amid a sharp US-Iran standoff. US officials say Iran privately told them that an attack on ships transiting the strait earlier in the week was 'a mistake', as Trump traded threats with Iran's new supreme leader in the wake of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral. ⚠ Iran's characterization of the attack as 'a mistake' is relayed via unnamed US officials and not independently confirmed

item.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
item.description: Firefighters in southern Spain are fighting one of the country's deadliest wildfires on record, with at least 12 people killed and another 23 reported missing. Most of the dead are believed to be British and Belgian nationals along with one Spaniard, and crews near Almeria have shifted to an offensive strategy to try to bring the blaze under control. ⚠ Death and missing tolls are still being updated as the fire is fought

item.title: Landmark US housing bill becomes law despite Trump protest
item.description: A bipartisan bill aimed at lowering housing costs and boosting supply has become US law, with President Trump letting it take effect without his signature as a protest. Trump had wanted Republican voter-ID legislation passed first, and his refusal to sign highlights friction between the White House and members of his own party over legislative priorities.

item.title: India-New Zealand: साझा विकास के लिए रोडमैप और अन्य खबरें | Samachar
item.description: India's public broadcaster leads its bulletin on India and New Zealand agreeing on a roadmap for shared development and stronger bilateral ties, framed around a major goal for the relationship, alongside other national headlines. ⚠ State broadcaster — coverage foregrounds the government's framing of the India-NZ engagement

item.title: पीएम मोदी के प्रेस कॉन्फ़्रेंस न करने को लेकर अब न्यूज़ीलैंड में पूछा गया सवाल
item.description: After a similar question in Norway, a New Zealand journalist has now pressed PM Narendra Modi over his practice of not holding open press conferences during official visits. India's foreign ministry responded to the query, and the exchange has drawn attention to Modi's media accessibility on the same NZ trip that state media covers as a diplomatic success. ⚠ Independent framing highlights press-access criticism that state coverage of the NZ visit omits

item.title: 美国再次在联合国安理会否决加沙停火决议草案
item.description: People's Daily reports that the US again used its veto in the UN Security Council to block a draft resolution demanding an immediate, unconditional and permanent ceasefire in Gaza, the release of all detainees, and removal of restrictions on humanitarian access. Fourteen of the 15 members voted in favor, with the US the sole no vote. ⚠ State media ⚠ Stale-dated: article is timestamped June 2025 and appears to have resurfaced in the scan rather than being fresh

item.title: На Тайване 87 человек пострадали из-за тайфуна "Бави"
item.description: Russian state agency RIA Novosti reports that 87 people were injured in Taiwan as Typhoon Bavi passed over the island before moving toward mainland China, where authorities are evacuating residents ahead of landfall. ⚠ Russian state media

item.title: В Сумах в результате российского удара погибли четыре человека. Еще 17 получили ранения
item.description: Independent outlet Meduza reports that a daytime Russian strike on the center of Sumy on 11 July killed four people and wounded 17, according to the head of the regional civil administration, Oleh Hryhorov.

item.title: 高市首相“安倍元首相にならい新たな挑戦続けていく”姿勢示す
item.description: At an event marking four years since the assassination of former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe, Prime Minister Takaichi said Abe took on divisive national issues head-on and signaled her intent to keep pursuing new challenges in the same spirit.

item.title: 東京ドームシティ、従業員死亡事故 「組織的な問題」調査委が指摘
item.description: An investigative committee concluded that 'organizational problems' contributed to a fatal accident that killed a worker at the Tokyo Dome City entertainment complex, pointing to systemic failures rather than isolated error.

item.title: Tulloch to be resentenced for Zantop murders
item.description: Robert Tulloch, 43, will be resentenced and is seeking an alternative to the two concurrent life-without-parole sentences he is serving. As a teenager from Chelsea, Vermont, he and a friend killed a pair of Dartmouth College professors in one of the region's most notorious murder cases.

item.title: Cae uno de los más buscados por la DEA en un operativo en San Juan
item.description: Police captured Luis Amézquita Falú, described as one of the DEA's most-wanted fugitives, in an operation in San Juan. According to authorities, he is suspected in several killings in the San Juan and Carolina areas.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, accuses ex-employees of stealing trade secrets
item.description: Apple has filed suit against OpenAI, accusing former Apple employees who moved to the AI company of stealing trade secrets. The case escalates the rivalry between the two companies as they increasingly compete over on-device AI and consumer products.

item.title: China recovered its first reusable rocket and showed a new way to do it
item.description: China has recovered a reusable rocket for the first time, demonstrating an approach to landing and reuse that observers say clearly draws inspiration from SpaceX while introducing some novel techniques of its own. The milestone marks a significant step in China's push toward lower-cost, reusable launch capability.

item.title: A Jupiter-size planet that escaped its star's death
item.description: Astronomers have found a Jupiter-size planet that appears to have survived its host star's transformation into a bloated red giant, a stage that normally engulfs nearby worlds. How the planet avoided destruction remains unclear and challenges assumptions about planetary survival around dying stars.

item.title: White House taps the guy who keeps crying 'aliens' to run UFO group
item.description: Harvard astrophysicist Avi Loeb, known for his repeated speculation about extraterrestrial origins for anomalous objects, will head a new UAP Science Advisory Council established by the White House together with the Pentagon, ODNI, the FBI and the intelligence community. The council is tasked with providing scientific advice to a UAP Governing Board aiming to 'resolve the nature of UAP.'

item.title: Crunchyroll Reveals Cast, Staff for Same-Day English Dub of Hanaori-san Still Wants to Fight in the Next Life Anime
item.description: Crunchyroll has announced the cast and staff for a same-day English dub of the anime 'Hanaori-san Still Wants to Fight in the Next Life,' with Kelly Greenshield and Mauricio Ortiz-Segura among the leads. The dub debuts Saturday alongside the subtitled release.

item.title: A Chat with Wistoria: Wand and Sword Season 2 Director, Hideaki Nakano
item.description: In an interview, Hideaki Nakano discusses stepping into the director's chair for the second season of 'Wistoria: Wand and Sword,' what he loves about the show, and his approach to the new season.

item.title: US and Iran exchange intensifying fire across Mideast, threatening ceasefire deal
item.description: Intensifying military exchanges between the United States and Iran across the Middle East are threatening to unravel the ceasefire brokered last year, with the fighting spilling over into shipping and oil routes in the region. ⚠ Fast-moving, active conflict — operational and casualty details remain fluid and contested

item.title: Andy Burnham poised to become Britain's next PM after most Labour lawmakers support him
item.description: Andy Burnham is on the verge of becoming Britain's next prime minister after an overwhelming show of support from Labour lawmakers left him all but certain to replace Keir Starmer.

item.title: 8 men indicted in planned drone and sniper attack on White House UFC cage-fighting show
item.description: US prosecutors indicted eight men over an alleged plot to attack a planned UFC cage-fighting event at the White House using drones and a sniper. ⚠ Charges are allegations that have not been tested in court

item.title: अभिषेक बनर्जी को हाई कोर्ट की फटकार, अन्य खबरें: News Capsule
item.description: A news-bulletin segment from India's state broadcaster whose lead item reports that a High Court reprimanded Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee, alongside other headlines of the day. ⚠ Indian state broadcaster — framing reflects the government's perspective

item.title: भारत अपना अहम हथियार ब्रह्मोस कई देशों को क्यों बेच रहा है, रूस की परमिशन क्या ज़रूरी है?
item.description: BBC Hindi examines why India is striking deals to export its prized BrahMos supersonic cruise missile to several countries, and whether Russia — its co-developer — must sign off on such sales.

item.title: 商务部：中方对合规稀土出口许可申请将予以批准
item.description: China's Ministry of Commerce said it will approve compliant applications for rare-earth export licenses, framing export controls on these dual-use materials as standard international practice while pledging to facilitate lawful, compliant trade. ⚠ Chinese state media — framing reflects the government's position ⚠ People's Daily world feed appears infrequently updated; this item is dated June 2025

item.title: В США дали название новому витку военной операции против Ирана, пишут СМИ
item.description: Russian state agency RIA Novosti reports, citing media, that the United States has assigned a name to a new phase of its military operation against Iran. ⚠ Russian state-media source — treat framing with caution

item.title: «Би-би-си»: на войне России с Украиной погибли более 3500 иностранцев, завербованных в российские войска
item.description: Independent outlet Meduza, citing a BBC and Mediazona investigation that maintains a named casualty list, reports that more than 3,500 foreigners recruited into Russian forces have been killed in Russia's full-scale war against Ukraine. ⚠ Figure comes from an ongoing journalistic casualty tally, not official data

item.title: 【詳報】皇室典範改正案 衆院本会議で可決 今国会で成立の公算
item.description: Japan's Lower House passed a bill to amend the Imperial House Law aimed at securing the number of imperial family members, backed by the LDP, Ishin and other parties. It now moves to the Upper House and is likely to be enacted during the current Diet session.

item.title: 「検察なめんな」の元特捜検事、無罪を主張 「罪は成立しません」
item.description: A former Osaka District special-investigation prosecutor — charged with assault and cruelty by a special public official for banging the desk and berating a suspect during questioning ("don't mess with the prosecution") — pleaded not guilty at the opening of his trial, arguing his conduct did not amount to cruelty.

item.title: Content creator 'Planet Hank' sues Vermont attorney general over AI video probe
item.description: A Brattleboro content creator known as 'Planet Hank' is suing Vermont Attorney General Charity Clark, whose office said he may have violated a new state AI political-ad law by posting a video depicting U.S. Rep. Becca Balint being thrown into a dumpster by a political opponent.

item.title: Drought and water-service outages threaten Puerto Rico's agricultural sector
item.description: Drought and interruptions to water service are threatening Puerto Rico's farm sector. The Agriculture Department has not yet reported losses, but experts warn that if conditions persist, production and the agri-food supply chain will suffer.

item.title: OpenAI announces GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6, the newest iteration of its flagship model; the release drew heavy discussion on Hacker News.

item.title: Humanoid robots controlled by surgeons did world-first operation on live pigs
item.description: Surgeons remotely controlling humanoid robots performed what researchers describe as a world-first operation on live pigs, a preclinical trial testing whether humanoid robots are feasible in surgery.

item.title: For the First Time, a Cell Built From Scratch Grows and Divides
item.description: Scientists built a synthetic cell from scratch that both grows and divides, combining more lifelike properties than any previous artificial cell — a proof of concept that nonliving materials can be brought close to life in the lab.

item.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
item.description: The New York Times alleges OpenAI feigned an inability to search its training data and hid or deleted billions of ChatGPT logs — conduct the paper says could expose OpenAI to sanctions in the ongoing copyright suit. ⚠ Claims are the New York Times' litigation allegations; OpenAI disputes them

item.title: Live-Action Happy Kanako's Killer Life Series Gets 2nd Season
item.description: The live-action adaptation of "Happy Kanako's Killer Life" is getting a second season, set to debut in September.

item.title: Crunchyroll Streams Same-Day English Dub for 'Goodbye, Lara' Anime
item.description: Crunchyroll will stream a same-day English dub for the 'Goodbye, Lara' anime, with a voice cast that includes Brianna Knickerbocker and Anairis Quiñones.

item.title: Armiger Entertainment Launches Kickstarter Campaign for Tenchi Galaxy Anime Pilot
item.description: Armiger Entertainment launched a Kickstarter to fund a 'Tenchi Galaxy' anime pilot, with Tenchi Universe director Hiroshi Negishi returning for a spiritual sequel he hopes to expand into a full new series.

item.title: #663 2C or Not 2C
item.description: The latest episode of the comedy podcast with Mark Normand and Joe List, riffing on Joe's run-in with a 'human speed bump' on a trip and Mark's new scheme for flying next to a large middle-seat passenger.

item.title: RIDICULOUS - 2026 Special - Trailer #1
item.description: Louis C.K. released the first trailer for his 2026 stand-up special, 'Ridiculous.'

item.title: #484 — Artificial Intimacy
item.description: Sam Harris speaks with Paul Bloom about our relationships with AI and the future of human connection — the pace of AI progress, AI companions as a remedy for loneliness, digitally resurrecting the dead, and whether AI can be conscious.

item.title: About Graham Platner Dropping Out and the "Missing Middle"
item.description: The hosts discuss Graham Platner's exit from the Maine Senate race following a sexual assault allegation and what comes next for Maine Democrats, then turn to the shrinking 45-64 'missing middle' and its potential fallout for society and the economy.

item.title: Episode 335: The Wright Stuff
item.description: David and Tamler host Robert Wright to discuss his new book on artificial intelligence and existential risk, debating the magnitude of AI's potential impact and a natural-selection analogy Wright presses in the book.

item.title: US and Iran trade strikes for second night in a row after Trump declares ceasefire 'over'
item.description: [news] (BBC World) Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped 'dramatically' since the lastest attacks began.

item.title: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
item.description: [news] (BBC World) The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

item.title: Platner's disastrous candidacy exposes rifts that could dampen Democrats' Senate hopes
item.description: [news] (BBC World) Along with creating tumult in a must-win race for Democrats, Graham Platner's exit is laying bare division between the party's left wing and moderates.

item.title: It was 'love at first sight' with their adopted baby. Then they were told he may have been trafficked
item.description: [news] (BBC World) When they adopted Marcus, David and Ally's dream of having a child was complete - but now they could lose him.

item.title: Watch: We're a step closer to finding out if bees have feelings, here's why
item.description: [news] (BBC World) New research found bumblebees showed "emotion-like behaviours" previously only seen in mammals.

item.title: International court tells BBC of breakthrough in Sudan war crimes probe
item.description: [news] (BBC World) The ICC has been looking into atrocities committed in Darfur over the past three years.

item.title: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
item.description: [news] (BBC World) Although the Patriots are highly effective against Russia's ballistic missiles, they also have lengthy production times.

item.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
item.description: [news] (BBC World) The weapon has not been brought back to the UK and has instead been left with British officials in Turkey.

item.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' has died at 75
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Tyler died "unexpectedly" in a hospital in Portugal where she was being treated for an illness, her family said Thursday in a statement on her website.

item.title: A Florida airport is officially renamed for Trump. What does he stand to gain?
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Trump is the first president to have an airport named after him while in office. The Trump Organization says he won't get royalties from the renaming, but legal experts see potential loopholes.

item.title: New aviation mechanics graduate with jobs in hand, thanks to a labor shortage
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.

item.title: Campaign staffers keep trying to bet on races despite push to curb insider trading
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Kalshi says it has blocked "dozens" of trades from campaign insiders, but experts say the company's approach leaves lots of potential loopholes. NPR has found at least one trade that slipped through.

item.title: The Iran war has pushed some countries away from oil and toward clean energy
item.description: [news] (NPR News) The new round of bombing in the Middle East has underscored the precarity of relying on fossil fuels. The war is speeding up the global transition to EVs, solar, and batteries, experts say.

item.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
item.description: [news] (NPR News) As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: [news] (NPR News) Trump criticizes Iran's leaders and says ceasefire is over, Middle East countries prepare for the potential of more war as U.S. and Iran renew strikes, Graham Platner drops his bid for Senate.

item.title: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along ΓÇô it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven ΓÇ£just as effective as DeetΓÇ¥ as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria ΓÇô nepetalactone ΓÇô also has insect-repelling properties but this has not previously been commercialised. Continue reading...

item.title: What will define Elon MuskΓÇÖs legacy? Doge cuts to USAID Ebola programs
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Experts say cuts have hindered the response to DRCΓÇÖs Ebola outbreak and resulted in ΓÇÿsignificant numbersΓÇÖ of deathsElon Musk has an Ebola problem. SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. But instead of focusing on his companies, Musk has posted frequently on X about the US Agency for International Development (USAID), which he helped dismantle ΓÇô or, in his words, feed into the woodchipper ΓÇô last year.ΓÇ£ElonΓÇÖs USAID crash-out over the past week has been a thing to behold,ΓÇ¥ said Jeremy Konyndyk, a former top USAID official who oversaw the agencyΓÇÖs Ebola response in 2014-2015 and the president of Refugees International. ΓÇ£In a way, itΓÇÖs helpful that Elon is doing this, because itΓÇÖs putting attention back on the issue of what he did last year.ΓÇ¥ Continue reading...

item.title: Five charged in Liberia after more than 200kg of cocaine seized in drug bust
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Shipment discovered at airport in Monrovia and valued at ┬ú14.2m had been falsely declared as seasoning cubesAuthorities in Liberia have charged five suspects over one of the largest drug seizures in the countryΓÇÖs history, after police found more than 200kg of cocaine falsely declared as Maggi seasoning cubes.The shipment, with an estimated value of $19m (┬ú14.2m), was discovered at the international airport in Monrovia on 8 June, but the suspects were not named until a press briefing at the weekend. Continue reading...

item.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in EgyptΓÇÖs western desert
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activitiesArchaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert.The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-style church in the Dakhla oasis. Archaeologists also found coins, pottery fragments and tools. Continue reading...

item.title: ΓÇÿThe situation is terribleΓÇÖ: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between SudanΓÇÖs armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSFFatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far.The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. ΓÇ£Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,ΓÇ¥ said the aid volunteer, whose name has been changed for fear of retribution. Continue reading...

item.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacySome of the UKΓÇÖs top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men.The countryΓÇÖs ΓÇ£buggery lawΓÇ¥, often referred to as its ΓÇ£sodomyΓÇ¥ law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and TobagoΓÇÖs 1986 Sexual Offences Act. In 2017 a Trinidadian LGBTQ+ rights activist, Jason Jones, challenged the law, and in 2018 a high court ruled that it infringed upon his constitutional right to privacy and equality. Continue reading...

item.title: French prosecutors investigate racist abuse of Kylian Mbapp├⌐ by Paraguayan senator
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Celeste Amarilla could face charges after French Football Federation complains about social media posts over World Cup matchProsecutors in France have opened an investigation into the racist attack on Kylian Mbapp├⌐ by a Paraguayan senator, with officials weighing whether to demand that the senator be charged with aggravated public insult or incitement to hatred or violence.The Paris prosecutorΓÇÖs office told the Guardian on Tuesday it had launched the inquiry after the French Football Federation (FFF) filed a complaint with the national unit for combating online hate. Continue reading...

item.title: ΓÇÿLiving like this is agonyΓÇÖ: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in six months
item.description: [news] (The Guardian (World)) Impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US imposed a blockade in JanuaryCuba on Monday suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, the state electricity company said.The impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US president, Donald Trump, imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for CubaΓÇÖs power plants. Continue reading...

item.title: UK Labour leadership nominations begin: WhoΓÇÖs running and how it works
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is the frontrunner. If no rival emerges, he could become PM this month.

item.title: India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) India floods sweep away thousands of gas cylinders

item.title: Deadly US strikes trigger Iranian attacks on Gulf states
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) The US military says it has struck 90 targets in Iran. Iran says it sent drone strikes on US-linked sites in the Gulf.

item.title: Iran prepares to bury slain leader Khamenei after mass funeral processions
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) The late supreme leader will be buried in his hometown, the eastern holy city of Mashhad.

item.title: US strikes Iran for second night ΓÇô is the peace process all over now?
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) Neither side has officially called off the interim peace agreement, despite the escalation.

item.title: UN probe finds mass killings, gang rapes by SudanΓÇÖs RSF amount to genocide
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) UN Fact-Finding Mission found that the paramilitary's systematic campaign of violence in Darfur amounted to genocide.

item.title: Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) Australia to begin selling uranium to India for peaceful purposes

item.title: Air raid sirens in Bahrain after uneasy night of US-Iran strikes
item.description: [news] (Al Jazeera English) After a tense night of overnight US-led strikes on Iran, air raid alerts sounded across Gulf states.

item.title: New attacks raise questions about what comes next in the Iran war
item.description: [news] (AP News)

item.title: US and Iran exchange intensifying fire across the Gulf
item.description: [news] (AP News)

item.title: Bitterly divided Iran grapples with KhameneiΓÇÖs legacy
item.description: [news] (AP News)

item.title: Trump flies partway home from Turkey in an old Air Force One, not the new Qatari-gifted jet
item.description: [news] (AP News)

item.title: E. Jean Carroll case, Platner to drop Senate bid, inside APΓÇÖs on-the-ground Ukraine coverage
item.description: [news] (AP News)

item.title: Graham Platner plans to withdraw from Maine Senate race after sexual assault claim

item.title: 474: Liz and Myq Give Advice
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) Liz Glazer is my wonderful friend. I'm my wonderful me. Together, we make a team of people who talk to each other every Wednesday on Instagram. This week I recorded us for this podcast, which we're calling (for now) "Liz and Myq Give Advice." We do that. Mostly to me. But also to other people! Like you in the future? Thanks for listening if you want to!

item.title: 473: Don't Rock the Cazh Boat (Or Do If You Want, Totally Cazh Here)
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) On today's Hang Out With Me, Myq appreciates that you are here, discusses the alphabet, encourages folks to vote, and as always, wonders why people continue to download a podcast that doesn't exist? There hasn't been a podcast here in twoooooooo years! Happy Halloween!

item.title: 472: All My Podcasts
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) Today, Myq continues his investigation of why you, the listener, are here, continuing to listen to a podcast that stopped being produced several years ago, but now is rising from the dead much like a [whatever dead-riser suits your tastes]. Please enjoy, and let me know why you're here! And thanks for considering listening to The Faucet, and Broccoli and Ice Cream, two of Myq's podcasts which ARE currently being produced and perpetuated sincerely on a weekly-plus basis! You're nice, thank you!

item.title: 471: The Center of the Night
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) Today, Myq asks why everyone is here as it gets later and later into the night. He's happy that you're here. He's also simply curious. There hasn't been a podcast here in *spooky ghost voice* THIRTY MONTHS! And yet you are still listening. Much gratitude. And curiosity! Please enjoy, if you dare!

item.title: 470: For Your #GramMYQ Consideration
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) On the latest episode of "Why are you still listening to this podcast that doesn't exist anymore? Ok, it still kind of does," Myq shares today's exciting announcement: His latest standup album "A.K.A." has been submitted to be considered to be nominated for a Best Comedy Album Grammy┬«! As they say, it's an honor to be submitted to be considered to be nominated. Thanks for listening and spreading the good #GramMYQ word!

item.title: 469: Are you still here? Go listen to the Faucet! Ok fine listen to this one, too.
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) This is Myq's old podcast. The last episode came out in 2018. Until last week. The Faucet is Myq's new podcast. He started releasing them this year, and wants everyone to know it. Myq just learned recently that people are still downloading THIS old podcast, despite there being no new episodes in 2.5 years. So, he's releasing new episodes here to encourage people to listen to his other podcasts, the Faucet and Broccoli & Ice Cream. So if you're here, go there! And thanks! Enjoy what you enjoy!

item.title: 468: Listen to my NEW new podcast called The Faucet!
item.description: [comedy] (Myq Kaplan - Hang Out With Me) In 2012, this podcast began. In 2018, this podcast un-began. In 2018, the new Myq Kaplan podcast Broccoli and Ice Cream began. In 2020, Myq realized that people were still downloading and listening to THIS podcast, so it UN-unbegan. It's back! At least this once. If only to let you know, there's a NEW new podcast as of this year, 2020! It's called The Faucet and I invite you to listen!
